frameworks.name: population-level dimensionality reduction and decoding
frameworks.one_line: isolate population activity patterns linked to stimulus, action, and motion

frameworks.name: sensorimotor linking via mPFC
frameworks.one_line: mPFC posited to bridge sensory inputs and learned behavioral outputs

frameworks.name: goal-directed action selection
frameworks.one_line: mPFC contributes to choosing between distinct learned avoidance actions

core_question: How does medial prefrontal cortex population activity link tone stimuli to selection among learned avoidance actions?
key_assumptions: population calcium imaging captures behaviorally relevant mPFC activity
stimulus, action, and motion signals are separable subspaces in population activity
active avoidance is a goal-directed learned behavior
manipulations: tone-signaled active avoidance paradigm in mice
two distinct learned avoidance actions to compare
key_findings: tone-related population activity was not informative about behavior
avoidance-related activity predicted upcoming avoidance actions
avoidance-related activity distinguished between two different learned avoidance actions
interpretations: mPFC population activity supports selection between different goal-directed actions rather than simple stimulus encoding
